records.ety: [OE. fasoun, facioun, shape, manner, F. facon, orig., a making, fr. L. factio a making, fr. facere to make. See Fact, Feat, and cf. Faction.]

defs: The make or form of anything; the style, shape, appearance, or mode of structure; pattern, model; as, the fashion of the ark, of a coat, of a house, of an altar, etc.; workmanship; execution.
quotes: The fashion of his countenance was altered.
I do not like the fashion of your garments.

defs: The prevailing mode or style, especially of dress; custom or conventional usage in respect of dress, behavior, etiquette, etc.; particularly, the mode or style usual among persons of good breeding; as, to dress, dance, sing, ride, etc., in the fashion.
quotes: As now existing, fashion is a form of social regulation analogous to constitutional government as a form of political regulation.

defs: Polite, fashionable, or genteel life; social position; good breeding; as, men of fashion.
Mode of action; method of conduct; manner; custom; sort; way.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Fashioned (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Fashioning.]

defs: To form; to give shape or figure to; to mold.
quotes: Here the loud hammer fashions female toys.
Ingenious art . . . Steps forth to fashion and refine the age.

defs: To fit; to adapt; to accommodate; -- with to.
quotes: Laws ought to be fashioned to the manners and conditions of the people.

defs: To make according to the rule prescribed by custom.
quotes: Fashioned plate sells for more than its weight.
